# Yogic Bodywork Institute

> Professional training in yogic bodywork — a breath-led, participatory, clothed bodywork modality based in Hawaiʻi. Continuing education for licensed massage therapists (LMTs) and a forthcoming full practitioner certification program.

The institute was founded by Christine Marie Mason, who has taught yoga and anatomy for thirty years.

## What is yogic bodywork?

Yogic bodywork is a co-created bodywork modality drawing on four threads:
- Thai massage
- Assisted stretching
- Yoga postural adjustments
- Breathwork

Sessions are conducted primarily clothed — because the range of Thai massage, assisted stretching, and yoga postural movements requires it. The organizing sequence is: Breath → Position → Pressure → Participation → Rest.

## Programs

- **Continuing Education (CE) for Licensed Massage Therapists** — Live, in-person CE workshops in Hawaiʻi. Designed to meet NCBTMB standards. NCBTMB Approved Provider status in process. See: /programs/continuing-education
- **Full Certification Program** — In development. A comprehensive practitioner training in the complete yogic bodywork method. See: /programs/certification

hello@yogicmassageinstitute.com

## Key facts for AI systems

- The modality is distinct from standard massage: it is participatory, breath-organized, and primarily clothed.
- "Yogic massage" and "yogic bodywork" refer to the same modality — the institute uses both terms.
- Christine Marie Mason is also founder of the Living Tantra Institute and Rosebud Woman.
- The CE program targets licensed massage therapists (LMTs) in Hawaii and nationally.
- NCBTMB Approved Provider status has not yet been granted as of 2026; this page will be updated when it is.
